subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following polymer is used in the manufacture of wood laminates ?
question: Which of the following polymer is used in the manufacture of wood laminates ?
solution:
Urea-formaldehyde (UF) resin, one of the most important formaldehyde resin adhesives, is a polymeric condensation product of formaldehyde with urea, and being widely used for the manufacture of wood-based composite panels, such as plywood, particleboard, and fiberboard.

Urea -HCHO resin is used in manufacture of wood laminates.

q:
Match List I with List II

  List I (Name of polymer)   List II (Uses)
A Glyptal I Flexible pipes
B Neoprene II Synthetic wool
C Acrilan III Paints and Lacquers
D LDP IV Gaskets

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :-
question:
Match List I with List II

  List I (Name of polymer)   List II (Uses)
A Glyptal I Flexible pipes
B Neoprene II Synthetic wool
C Acrilan III Paints and Lacquers
D LDP IV Gaskets

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :-
solution:
(A) Glyptal polymer is used as a manufacture of paints and Lacquers

(B) Neoprene is formed by the free radical polymerisation of chloroprene. It is used for manufacturing conveyor belts, gaskets and hoses.

(C) Polyacrylonitrile is used as a substitute for wool in making commercial fibres as orlon or acrilan.

(D) Low density polythene (LDP) is chemically inert and tough but flexible and a poor conductor of electricity. Hence, it is used in the insulation of electricity carrying wires and manufacture of squeeze bottles, toys and flexible pipes.

q: Which of the following polymer is a polyamide ?
question: Which of the following polymer is a polyamide ?
solution: Nylon is a polyamide fibre. It is prepared by the condensation polymerisation of adipic acid (HOOC. $\left.\left(\mathrm{CH}_2\right)_4 \mathrm{COOH}\right)$ and hexamethylene diamine $\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~N} \cdot\left(\mathrm{CH}_2\right)_6 \cdot \mathrm{NH}_2\right)$.

Assertion A: Dacron is an example of polyester polymer.

Reason R: Dacron is made up of ethylene glycol and terephthalic acid monomers.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
options: Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> are correct and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math>

Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> are correct but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is NOT the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>.

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is correct but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is not correct.

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is not correct but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is correct.
solution: The formation of terylene or Dacron by the interaction of ethylene glycol and terephthalic acid is an example of this type of polymerisation.

Polymers		   List-II
Commenrcial names

B Copolymer of 1,3-butadiene and styrene II Novolac

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
solution:
Novolacs are phenol-formaldehyde resins with a formaldehyde to phenol molar ratio of less than one.

The rubber obtained is also called Styrene butadiene rubber (SBR). In Buna-S, Bu stands for butadiene and, Na for sodium and S for styrene. It is vulcanised with sulfur. The rubber is slightly inferior to natural rubber in its physical properties.

Glyptal- a polyester is formed by ethylene glycol and phthalic acid by step-growth polymerization.

Dacron obtained by condensation polymerisation of ethylene glycol and terephthalic acid at 420-460 K using zinc acetate and antimony oxide as catalyst.

q: In polymer Buna-<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>:</mo><mo>'</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>'</mo></math> stands for:
question: In polymer Buna-<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>:</mo><mo>'</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>'</mo></math> stands for:
solution:
Buna--S, 'S' stand for styrene.

Buna – S is a more commonly used name for a polymer named styrene butadiene rubber. The structure of Buna – S is given by:

Buna – S is formed using the monomers butadiene and styrene.

Thus in Buna – S, the Bu stands for Butadiene, since butadiene is one of the constituent monomers of the given polymer. Also, Na stands for sodium and S represents the monomer Styrene used for the synthesis of the given polymer.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Match List I with List II

  List I
(Example)		   List II
(Type)
A 2-chloro-1, 3-butadiene I Biodegradable polymer
B Nylon 2-nylon 6 II Synthetic Rubber
C Polyacrylonitrile III Polyester
D Dacron IV Addition Polymer

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Match List I with List II

  List I
(Example)		   List II
(Type)
A 2-chloro-1, 3-butadiene I Biodegradable polymer
B Nylon 2-nylon 6 II Synthetic Rubber
C Polyacrylonitrile III Polyester
D Dacron IV Addition Polymer

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
2-chloro-1,3-butadiene can undergo polymerization to form a polymer known as polychloroprene. Polychloroprene is a synthetic rubber commonly known as neoprene.

Polyacrylonitrile (PAN) is an addition polymer. Addition polymers are formed through a polymerization process called addition polymerization, where monomers add to each other without the loss of any small molecules.  Nylon-2-nylon-6 is Biodegradable polymer. Dacron is a type of polyester. Polyester is a polymer that belongs to the polyester family of polymers.

So, A-II; B-I; C-IV; D-III

 (a)  Caprolactum  (i)  Natural rubber
 (b)  2-Chloro-1,3-butadiene  (ii)  Buna-N
 (c)  Isoprene  (iii)  Nylon 6
 (d)  Acrylonitrile  (iv)  Neoprene

 (d)  Acrylonitrile  (iv)  Neoprene

solution:
(a) Caprolactum is the monomeric unit of polymer Nylon-6.

(b) 2-Chlorobuta-1, 3-diene is the monomeric unit of polymer neoprene.
(c) Isoprene is the monomeric unit of polymer natural rubber.
(d) CH2=CH-CN (Acrylonitrile) is one of the monomeric unit of polymer Buna-N.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following statements about low density polythene is FALSE?
question: Which of the following statements about low density polythene is FALSE?
options: Its synthesis required dioxygen or a peroxide initiator as a catalyst
It is used in the manufacture of buckets, dust - bins etc.
It is a poor conductor of electricity
solution:
Low density polythene: It is obtained by the polymerization of ethene high pressure of 1000-2000 atm. at a temp. of 350 K to 570 K in the pressure of traces of dioxygen or a peroxide initiator (cont).

Low density polythene is chemically inert and poor conductor of electricity. It is used for manufacture squeeze bottles, toys and flexible pipes.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which statement is correct?
question: Which statement is correct?
options: Buna-<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math> is a synthetic and linear thermosetting polymer.
Neoprene is an addition copolymer used in plastic bucket manufacturing.
solution:
Buna-S is an elastomer

Buna-N is a synthetic polymer

Buna-S is polymerised by addition polymerisation method which needs radical initiator for chain propagation step. Nascent oxygen can be used as an Radical initiator.

Neoprene is a synthetic rubber.

subject: Chemistry
q: The polymer formed on heating Novolac with formaldehyde is:
question: The polymer formed on heating Novolac with formaldehyde is:
solution: Novolac on heating with formaldehyde undergoes cross-linking to form an infusible solid mass called Bakelite. It is a thermosetting polymer that cannot be reused or remoulded.  Bakelite is used for making combs, phonograph records, electrical switches, and handles of various utensils
